findings.title: Write API duration naming hides that the metric spans the whole streaming upload session
findings.why: The new public metric and dashboard copy read like per-request API latency, but the write path is a multi-call streaming contract. That mismatch makes the write series look symmetric with `Ingest`, even though it measures a broader lifecycle.
findings.risk_if_unchanged: Operators and future maintainers can misread this as the latency of a single `Write` call and compare it directly with `Ingest` latency, which makes dashboards, alerts, and follow-up instrumentation decisions misleading.
findings.evidence: `WriteClient` explicitly allows multiple `Write` calls before completion. The timer starts once in `init` (`w.initTime = time.Now()`), the observation runs once when the chunked HTTP goroutine exits, and `TestWriteClientDurationMetricObserveOnce` writes two batches but asserts only one histogram sample. The exported metric/help text (`write_ingest_api_duration`, "write and ingest API duration") and Grafana title (`Write/Ingest API Duration`) do not communicate that broader write-session scope.
findings.change_request: Prefer `write_session_duration` or similarly explicit wording for the write-side metric/dashboard text; current name is confusing. If you want to keep one histogram family, add a short why-comment and help/title text that state the write bucket covers the full `WriteClient.init` -> `Recv` streaming session, not each `Write` call.
